events.text: Amanda Johnson, 45-year-old White female, Former smoker (quit 5 years ago). Lives independently. ECOG performance status 1.
Diagnosed with High-grade serous carcinoma of the Right ovary. Clinical staging: T3cN1M0, AJCC Stage IIIC. ICD-10: C56.9. Stage IIIC high-grade serous ovarian cancer with BRCA1 mutation.
PET/CT performed for baseline staging.
Pathology specimen received for initial biopsy.
Clinical visit for initial oncology consultation.
NGS report received for molecular profiling.

Underwent primary debulking surgery: Total abdominal hysterectomy, bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy, omentectomy, and optimal cytoreduction (residual disease < 1 cm). Estimated blood loss 300 mL. Length of stay 4 days. Uncomplicated postoperative course.
Pathology specimen received for surgical pathology.
Clinical visit for postoperative follow-up.

Initiated first-line chemotherapy with anti-angiogenic: Carboplatin AUC 6 + Paclitaxel 175 mg/m2 q3w x 6 cycles + Bevacizumab 15 mg/kg q3w. Cycle 1. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 2 fatigue. Activity modification recommended. TSH and CBC checked - within normal limits.

Initiated first-line chemotherapy with anti-angiogenic: Carboplatin AUC 6 + Paclitaxel 175 mg/m2 q3w x 6 cycles + Bevacizumab 15 mg/kg q3w. Cycle 2. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 2 assessment.
Developed grade 2 anemia. Hemoglobin 8.5 g/dL. Transfused 2 units pRBC. Iron studies checked.

Initiated first-line chemotherapy with anti-angiogenic: Carboplatin AUC 6 + Paclitaxel 175 mg/m2 q3w x 6 cycles + Bevacizumab 15 mg/kg q3w. Cycle 3. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 2 fatigue. Activity modification recommended. TSH and CBC checked - within normal limits.

Initiated first-line chemotherapy with anti-angiogenic: Carboplatin AUC 6 + Paclitaxel 175 mg/m2 q3w x 6 cycles + Bevacizumab 15 mg/kg q3w. Cycle 4. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 2 neuropathy. Dose reduction of neurotoxic agent by 25%. Gabapentin 300 mg TID started.
Clinical visit for cycle 4 assessment.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for restaging after first-line chemotherapy with anti-angiogenic.
Clinical visit for results review after first-line chemotherapy with anti-angiogenic.
Initiated PARP inhibitor maintenance: Olaparib 300 mg BID maintenance. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for follow-up on PARP inhibitor maintenance.
Clinical visit for follow-up on PARP inhibitor maintenance.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for surveillance imaging.
PET/CT performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.

documents.text:
PET/CT REPORT

Patient: Amanda Johnson
MRN: patient_d562b6a6e9d2
Date of Study: 2022-10-05

CLINICAL INDICATION: High-grade serous carcinoma of the right ovary. baseline staging.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Clear. No pulmonary nodules or masses. No pleural effusion.

MEDIASTINUM: No significant lymphadenopathy. Heart size normal.

LIVER: Homogeneous attenuation. No focal lesions.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: Complex cystic and solid mass in the right adnexa measuring 10.8 cm. Omental caking present. Moderate ascites. Peritoneal carcinomatosis.

BONES: No suspicious osseous lesions. Degenerative changes of the lumbar spine.

IMPRESSION:
1. Complex adnexal mass with peritoneal carcinomatosis and ascites, concerning for advanced ovarian malignancy.

Electronically signed by: Robert Chang, MD
Department of Radiology

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Amanda Johnson
MRN: patient_d562b6a6e9d2
Specimen ID: S2022-30061
Date of Procedure: 2022-10-08
Date of Report: 2022-10-13

SPECIMEN: Right ovary, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
High-grade serous carcinoma, poorly differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 3.9 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 0.3 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Present
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
PAX8: Positive
WT1: Positive
P53: Aberrant (overexpression)
CK7: Positive
CK20: Negative

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Amanda Johnson - Right ovary" is a gray-white tissue fragment measuring 3.9 x 2.7 x 1.9 cm. The cut surface reveals a firm tan mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A7.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a poorly differentiated neoplasm composed of nests of atypical cells with scant cytoplasm and irregular nuclear contours. Mitotic figures are occasionally seen (3 per 10 HPF). No necrosis is identified. The surrounding stroma shows fibrosis.

Electronically signed by: Sarah Chen, MD
Department of Pathology

INITIAL CONSULTATION - Medical Oncology

Patient: Amanda Johnson
MRN: patient_d562b6a6e9d2
Date: 2022-10-14
Provider: Dr. Carlos Mendez

CHIEF COMPLAINT: initial oncology consultation

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Johnson is a 45-year-old female who presents for initial evaluation of newly diagnosed high-grade serous carcinoma of the right ovary, IIIC. Patient presented with symptoms prompting workup. Stage IIIC high-grade serous ovarian cancer with BRCA1 mutation. Pathology confirmed high-grade serous carcinoma. Molecular testing has been sent. Patient reports progressive dyspnea and cough x 6 weeks.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
- peripheral vascular disease
- hypertension

SURGICAL HISTORY:
- Right knee arthroscopy (age 45)

SOCIAL HISTORY:
- Former smoker (quit 2 years ago)
- Alcohol: Rare alcohol use
- Occupation: Retired teacher
- Lives with partner

FAMILY HISTORY:
- Sister with ovarian cancer at age 55.

ALLERGIES: Penicillin (rash)

MEDICATIONS:
- Lisinopril 10 mg daily

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: intermittent nausea, constipation, unintentional weight loss, dry mouth. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 143/67, HR 80, RR 15, Temp 97.4F, SpO2 95% on RA, Wt 84.6 kg
General: Comfortable, thin-appearing, no acute distress.
HEENT: No scleral icterus. Oropharynx clear.
Neck: No thyromegaly. No lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Clear to auscultation bilaterally.
CV: RRR, S1/S2 normal. No murmurs, gallops, or rubs.
Abdomen: Soft, non-distended. Well-healed midline surgical scar.
Extremities: No peripheral edema. Good pulses bilaterally.
Neuro: Alert and oriented. No focal neurological deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 3.4, Hgb 10.3, Plt 250, Cr 0.93

IMAGING:
See separate imaging report.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
45-year-old female with IIIC high-grade serous carcinoma of the right ovary. initial oncology consultation.

1. High-grade serous carcinoma of the right ovary, Stage IIIC
   - Reviewed pathology and imaging with patient and family
   - Recommend primary debulking surgery
   - Will discuss at multidisciplinary tumor board
   - Molecular profiling results pending
2. Supportive care
   - Nutrition consultation placed
   - Social work referral for psychosocial support
   - Peripheral access adequate
3. Follow-up in 1-2 weeks to initiate treatment

Electronically signed by: Amanda Foster, MD
Medical Oncology

NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

Patient: Amanda Johnson
MRN: patient_d562b6a6e9d2
Date of Report: 2022-10-23
Specimen: Right ovary (Biopsy)
Diagnosis: High-grade serous carcinoma
Panel: Tempus xT (468 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 7.5 mutations/Mb (Low)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  BRCA1 p.E1836fs (c.5503delA) - Frameshift (Exon 22) [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  TP53 p.C176Y - Missense (Exon 5)

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  CDK12 p.T826A - VUS
  ARID1A p.D1850N - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  No significant copy number alterations detected

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - BRCA1 pathogenic variant: FDA-approved PARP inhibitors include olaparib, niraparib, rucaparib.
  - Platinum sensitivity expected.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 493x coverage.
Tumor content: 23%.

Electronically signed by: Andrew Patel, MD
Molecular Pathology Laboratory

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Amanda Johnson
MRN: patient_d562b6a6e9d2
Specimen ID: S2022-75822
Date of Procedure: 2022-11-08
Date of Report: 2022-11-12

SPECIMEN: Right ovary, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
High-grade serous carcinoma, poorly differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 6.1 cm
Margins: Positive at inked margin
Lymphovascular invasion: Present
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
PAX8: Positive
WT1: Positive
P53: Aberrant (overexpression)
CK7: Positive
CK20: Negative

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Amanda Johnson - Right ovary" is a gray-white tissue fragment measuring 6.1 x 4.3 x 3.0 cm. The cut surface reveals a firm yellow-tan mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A4.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a poorly differentiated neoplasm composed of cords and trabeculae of atypical cells with scant cytoplasm and irregular nuclear contours. Mitotic figures are identified (6 per 10 HPF). No necrosis is identified. The surrounding stroma shows desmoplastic reaction.

Electronically signed by: Emily Nakamura, MD
Department of Pathology

CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST REPORT

Patient: Amanda Johnson
MRN: patient_d562b6a6e9d2
Date of Study: 2024-03-18

CLINICAL INDICATION: High-grade serous carcinoma of the right ovary. surveillance imaging.

COMPARISON: Comparison made to prior study from approximately 3 months ago.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Clear. No pulmonary nodules or masses. No pleural effusion.

MEDIASTINUM: No significant lymphadenopathy. Heart size normal.

LIVER: Homogeneous attenuation. No focal lesions.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: Complex cystic and solid mass in the right adnexa measuring 11.5 cm. Omental caking present. Moderate ascites. Peritoneal carcinomatosis.

BONES: No suspicious osseous lesions. Degenerative changes of the lumbar spine.

IMPRESSION:
1. Complex adnexal mass with peritoneal carcinomatosis and ascites, concerning for advanced ovarian malignancy.
2. Overall assessment: Stable disease per RECIST 1.1.

Department of Radiology
